info.title: Converting VMware Virtual Machines to KVM with virt-v2v

abstract.para: virt-v2v converts virtual machines from VMware and other hypervisors to run on &kvm;. This guide covers the prerequisites, preparation, and conversion process, with a focus on converting Windows Server 2022 guests.

section.title: Overview
section.para: virt-v2v is a command-line tool for converting third-party virtual machines into a format that runs on &qemu;/&kvm;. It is part of the virt-v2v package and uses libguestfs under the hood.
&svirtualization; uses virt-v2v via the Forklift migration tool to convert VMware virtual machines. The same tool can be used directly on the command line for individual conversions.

note.title: Supported source formats
note.para: virt-v2v supports conversion from VMware (VMX/VMDK), Xen, libvirt, and physical machines. This section covers VMware to &kvm; conversion.

section.title: Prerequisites
section.para: Before converting a virtual machine, ensure the following requirements are met on the &kvm; host:

listitem.para: virt-v2v package installed:
guestfs-winsupport package installed (required for Windows guests):
&vmdp; ISO image. Download the latest &vmdpa; ISO from the &scc; or the SUSE download portal.
Source VMware VM files (VMX and VMDK) accessible on the &kvm; host, either locally or via NFS.
Sufficient disk space on the target host to store the converted VM disk images.

section.title: Preparing the VMDP drivers
section.para: For Windows guests, virt-v2v requires virtio drivers and Windows tools to be available on the host. The &vmdpa; ISO provides a helper script that sets up the correct directory structure.

section.title: Setting up VMDP drivers
section.para: Mount the &vmdpa; ISO and run the virt-v2v-vmdp.sh script from the v2v_scripts directory:
The script performs the following actions:

listitem.para: Installs virtio drivers to /usr/share/virtio-win/ in the directory layout expected by virt-v2v.
Places pvxsvc.exe and other required Windows tools in /usr/share/virt-tools/.
Creates placeholder files for the QEMU Guest Agent MSI and Balloon Server to suppress non-critical warnings.

important.title: Do not manually copy driver files
important.para: Manual extraction and copying of VMDP files to /usr/share/virt-tools/ or /usr/share/virtio-win/ is not recommended. The directory structure expected by virt-v2v on SLES 16 differs from earlier versions. Always use the virt-v2v-vmdp.sh script to ensure the correct layout.

section.title: Converting a VMware VM
section.para: Use the virt-v2v command to convert the VMware VM. The following example converts a Windows Server 2022 VM:
Key command options:

listitem.para: Input mode: read VMware VMX configuration file.
Output mode: write disk image and XML configuration to the local filesystem. Use -o libvirt to define the VM directly in libvirt (requires pre-configured storage pools).
Output disk format. qcow2 is recommended for space efficiency.
Output storage path. For -o local, this is a directory path. For -o libvirt, this is a libvirt storage pool name.
Map a VMware network name to a target bridge or libvirt network name. If omitted, the network device mapping may require manual adjustment in the generated XML.

section.title: Using libvirt output mode
section.para: To define the converted VM directly in libvirt, use -o libvirt:
The -os parameter specifies a libvirt storage pool name. Ensure the pool exists and has sufficient free space before running the conversion.

section.title: Post-conversion steps
section.para: After a successful conversion, follow these steps to complete the migration:

listitem.para: Start the converted VM. On first boot, the VM will install virtio drivers. The VM may reboot one or two times during this process.
After the VM stabilizes, power it off and change the video adapter to virtio (viogpudo) for optimal graphics performance. This can be done via virsh edit or the Virtual Machine Manager GUI.
Start the VM again. It will complete the driver installation and boot normally.
Verify that virtio drivers are active for disk, network, and graphics in the Windows Device Manager.

note.title: UEFI boot requirement
note.para: Windows Server 2022 and other modern Windows guests may require UEFI boot firmware on the target. virt-v2v detects this automatically and sets the appropriate firmware type. If the VM fails to boot, verify the firmware setting in the VM configuration.

section.title: Expected warnings during conversion
section.para: The following warnings are informational and can be safely ignored:

varlistentry.term: QEMU Guest Agent MSI not found

para.literal: virt-v2v: warning: QEMU Guest Agent MSI not found on tools ISO/directory.

listitem.para: &vmdpa; does not provide a standalone QEMU Guest Agent MSI package. The guest agent is included in the VMDP setup.exe and is installed automatically during first boot of the converted VM.

para.literal: virt-v2v: warning: Balloon Server (blnsvr.exe) not found on tools ISO/directory.

listitem.para: &vmdpa; uses pvxsvc.exe instead of blnsvr.exe to provide balloon functionality. The service is installed during the first boot of the converted VM.

varlistentry.term: Random seed could not be set

para.literal: virt-v2v: warning: random seed could not be set for this type of guest

listitem.para: This warning indicates that virt-v2v was unable to set a random seed for the guest. This does not affect the conversion result.

section.title: Hivex.Error during inspection
section.para: If virt-v2v fails with:
section.screen: virt-v2v: error: libguestfs error: inspect_os: internal error: inspect_os: unhandled exception thrown: Hivex.Error
section.para: This error occurs when libguestfs cannot parse the Windows registry hive files. Possible causes and resolutions:

listitem.para: Ensure you are running the latest version of virt-v2v and guestfs-winsupport.
Set the environment variable LIBGUESTFS_BACKEND=direct before running the conversion:
Verify that the source VMDK disk is not corrupted by running a disk check on the VMware VM before conversion.

section.title: Virtio drivers not found
section.para: If you see the warning:
section.screen: virt-v2v: warning: there are no virtio drivers available for this version of Windows (10.0 x86_64 Server win2k22). virt-v2v looks for drivers in /usr/share/virtio-win
section.para: This means virt-v2v could not find the virtio drivers for the detected Windows version. The guest will be configured with slower emulated devices instead of virtio.

Resolution: ensure the virt-v2v-vmdp.sh script has been run from the &vmdpa; ISO. The script installs drivers in the correct directory layout. On SLES 16, the expected driver path for Windows Server 2022 is /usr/share/virtio-win/drivers/amd64/2k22/.

important.title: Do not manually create symlinks as a workaround
important.para: While creating a symbolic link from 2k22 to another driver directory (e.g., win11) may suppress the warning, it is not a supported configuration. Always use the virt-v2v-vmdp.sh script with an up-to-date &vmdpa; ISO.

section.title: VM fails to boot after conversion (Windows Server 2019/2022/2025)
section.para: After converting Windows Server 2019, 2022, or 2025, the VM may fail to boot with an Inaccessible Boot Device error or a page fault blue screen.

This issue is caused by the virtio block driver installation path. On these Windows versions, the VMDP setup installs the updated virtio block driver to the driver store directory (\windows\system32\driverstore\filerepository) using DIRID 13, while removing the driver from \windows\system32\drivers (DIRID 12). Windows fails to locate the block driver on next boot.
Resolution: ensure you are using a recent &vmdpa; ISO version (2.5 or later) that includes the fix. The updated VMDP setup.exe copies the driver to both locations, allowing the VM to boot correctly.
If the VM fails to boot with an older VMDP version, you can manually copy the virtio block driver from the driver store to \windows\system32\drivers to restore boot capability.

section.title: Network mapping issues
section.para: If the --network mapping fails, the converted VM may not have a correctly configured network interface. To resolve:

listitem.para: Verify the source network name matches the network name defined in the VMware VMX file.
Ensure the target bridge or libvirt network exists on the host before conversion.
If using -o libvirt, create a libvirt network with a matching name using virsh net-create.

section.title: Debugging virt-v2v
section.para: To enable verbose output for debugging, run virt-v2v with the -v option:
For additional libguestfs debugging, set:

section.title: Complete example: Windows Server 2022
section.para: The following example demonstrates a full conversion of a Windows Server 2022 VM from VMware to &kvm;:
section.screen:
&prompt.sudo;mount -o loop /data/isos/vmdp/vmdp-VERSION.iso /mnt &prompt.sudo;/mnt/v2v_scripts/virt-v2v-vmdp.sh /mnt/vmdp-VERSION &prompt.sudo;umount /mnt
&prompt.user;export LIBGUESTFS_BACKEND=direct &prompt.sudo;virt-v2v -i vmx /srv/vmware/windows2022/windows2022.vmx -o libvirt -of qcow2 -os default --network VM Network:default
section.para: After conversion completes:

listitem.para: Start the VM: virsh start windows2022
Allow the VM to reboot (virtio driver installation)
Once stable, power off and switch video to virtio
Restart and verify all virtio drivers are active
